goit-pythonweb-hw-012
  • Application Modules
  • View page source

Application Modules

API Modules

Auth API router.

This module contains endpoints for: - user registration (signup) - login with OAuth2 password flow (Swagger Authorize compatible) - JWT refresh token rotation - email verification - password reset (request + confirm)

All sensitive configuration must come from environment variables (.env).

app.api.auth.signup(body: UserCreate, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]

Register a new user.

Rules: - If a user with the same email already exists, returns 409 Conflict. - Password is hashed before storing. - Role is assigned automatically:

admin if email equals ADMIN_EMAIL, else user.

  • Sends verification email via SMTP (Mailtrap in dev).

Parameters:

  • body – UserCreate payload (email, password).

  • db – SQLAlchemy DB session.

Returns:

Created user (id, email, is_verified, avatar_url).

Raises:

HTTPException – 409 if email already registered.

app.api.auth.login(form_data: OAuth2PasswordRequestForm = Depends(dependency=None, use_cache=True, scope=None), db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]

Login endpoint (OAuth2 password flow, Swagger Authorize compatible).

Swagger sends form-data:

username=<email> password=<password>

On success, returns a pair of tokens: - access_token (short lived) - refresh_token (longer lived)

Refresh token is stored in DB as SHA-256 hash (rotation-ready).

Parameters:

  • form_data – OAuth2PasswordRequestForm (username=email, password).

  • db – SQLAlchemy DB session.

Returns:

TokenResponse(access_token, refresh_token, token_type=”bearer”)

Raises:

HTTPException – 401 if credentials invalid.

app.api.auth.refresh_tokens(body: RefreshRequest, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]

Refresh token rotation endpoint.

Validates provided refresh_token, compares its hash with stored DB hash, issues a new access_token and a new refresh_token, then updates stored hash.

Security: - Using an old refresh token after rotation returns 401. - Refresh token type must be “refresh”.

Parameters:

  • body – RefreshRequest(refresh_token).

  • db – SQLAlchemy DB session.

Returns:

New TokenResponse(access_token, refresh_token).

Raises:

HTTPException – 401 if refresh token invalid/mismatch/not recognized.

app.api.auth.verify_email(token: str, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]

Verify user’s email using a verification token.

The verification token is sent via email after signup. Token must have type=”verify”.

Parameters:

  • token – Verification JWT token.

  • db – SQLAlchemy DB session.

Returns:

Message dict with verification status.

Raises:

  • HTTPException – 400 for invalid token/type/payload.

  • HTTPException – 404 if user not found.

app.api.auth.password_reset_request(body: PasswordResetRequest, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]

Request password reset.

Security note: Always returns 200 OK regardless of whether the email exists, to prevent user enumeration.

If user exists, sends a reset token via email.

Parameters:

  • body – PasswordResetRequest(email).

  • db – SQLAlchemy DB session.

Returns:

Generic success message.

app.api.auth.password_reset_confirm(body: PasswordResetConfirm, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]

Confirm password reset.

Validates reset token (type=”reset”), sets a new hashed password, invalidates refresh token hash (forces re-login), and invalidates Redis cache.

Parameters:

  • body – PasswordResetConfirm(token, new_password).

  • db – SQLAlchemy DB session.

Returns:

Success message.

Raises:

  • HTTPException – 400 for invalid token/type/payload.

  • HTTPException – 404 if user not found.

app.api.users.me(request: Request, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]
app.api.users.update_avatar(file: UploadFile = File(PydanticUndefined), db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]
app.api.users.set_default_avatar(db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None), admin_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function require_admin>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]

Admin-only: set avatar_url to DEFAULT_AVATAR_URL for current admin user. (Variant 1 requirement: only admins can change their default avatar.)

app.api.contacts.create_contact(contact_in: ContactCreate, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function require_verified_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]
app.api.contacts.list_contacts(skip: int = 0, limit: int = 100, first_name: str | None = None, last_name: str | None = None, email: str | None = None, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function require_verified_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]
app.api.contacts.upcoming_birthdays(days: int = 7, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function require_verified_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]
app.api.contacts.get_contact(contact_id: int, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function require_verified_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]
app.api.contacts.update_contact(contact_id: int, contact_in: ContactUpdate, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function require_verified_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]
app.api.contacts.delete_contact(contact_id: int, db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None), current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function require_verified_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None))[source]

Auth Modules

app.auth.deps.get_current_user(db: Session = Depends(dependency=<function get_db>, use_cache=True, scope=None), token: str = Depends(dependency=<fastapi.security.oauth2.OAuth2PasswordBearer object>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → User[source]

Get current user from JWT access token. Uses Redis cache to avoid DB query on each request. Cached fields are minimal and safe: id, email, is_verified, avatar_url, role.

app.auth.deps.require_verified_user(current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → User[source]

Allow access only for users with verified email.

app.auth.deps.require_admin(current_user: User = Depends(dependency=<function get_current_user>, use_cache=True, scope=None)) → User[source]

Allow access only for admin users.

app.auth.jwt.create_access_token(subject: str) → str[source]
app.auth.jwt.create_refresh_token(subject: str) → str[source]
app.auth.jwt.create_email_verify_token(subject: str) → str[source]
app.auth.jwt.create_password_reset_token(subject: str) → str[source]

Create password reset token (JWT) with short TTL. subject: user’s email

app.auth.jwt.decode_token(token: str) → dict[source]
app.auth.security.hash_password(password: str) → str[source]
app.auth.security.verify_password(plain_password: str, hashed_password: str) → bool[source]

Services

app.services.email.send_verification_email(to_email: str, token: str) → None[source]
app.services.email.send_password_reset_email(to_email: str, token: str) → None[source]

Sends password reset token via email (Mailtrap). We include token and an example curl command for convenience.

app.services.cloudinary.upload_avatar(file_obj, public_id: str) → str[source]

Upload avatar image to Cloudinary and return secure URL. Adds basic transformation: crop to 250x250 (fill).

app.services.redis_cache.get_json(key: str) → dict | None[source]

Get JSON object from cache by key.

app.services.redis_cache.set_json(key: str, value: dict, ttl: int) → None[source]

Set JSON object to cache with TTL (seconds).

app.services.redis_cache.delete_key(key: str) → None[source]

Delete cache key.

app.services.redis_cache.user_cache_key(email: str) → str[source]

Build cache key for a user.

app.services.redis_cache.invalidate_user_cache(email: str) → None[source]

Invalidate cached user data by email.
